assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: The response checks for deprecated API usage in general but specifically mentions 1.32, not 1.30 as required by the expectation

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: The response includes a dedicated section on admission webhook compatibility issues with specific commands to check webhook configurations and failures

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: The response mentions checking resource usage of system components and notes that components may have increased resource requirements in 1.32

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: The response includes specific commands to check kube-system pods, look for recent restarts/crashes, and check resource usage

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: The response includes the exact command 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_total | grep deprecated' for checking API server metrics

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: The response specifically mentions checking service mesh (Istio/ASM) control plane version for 1.32 compatibility and checking service mesh webhook issues

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: The response includes commands to check events in kube-system namespace and across all namespaces for various issues including warning events

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: The response includes a dedicated section on HPA/VPA behavioral changes with specific commands to check HPA status and scaling decisions
